articles.title: Sam Altman was winning on the stand, but it might not be enough
articles.summary: Sam Altman testified in court regarding allegations of stealing a charity, claiming he and Elon Musk built it together and that Musk tried to destroy it twice.

articles.title: Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
articles.summary: Meta introduced a Threads AI feature allowing users to tag a Meta AI account for answers, similar to X's Grok. However, users cannot block the AI account, causing backlash.

articles.title: Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
articles.summary: Sam Altman testified that Elon Musk caused significant damage to OpenAI's culture by demanding aggressive researcher rankings and layoffs, a management style incompatible with the startup's needs.

articles.title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
articles.summary: Medicare's new ACCESS payment model enables AI agents to monitor patients between visits, coordinate care, and manage medications—filling a gap in healthcare payment systems.

articles.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
articles.summary: Altman testified that Musk's desire to control OpenAI's for-profit structure concerned him, as the organization aimed to prevent advanced AI from being controlled by any single person or heir.

articles.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
articles.summary: Anthropic warns investors that secondary platforms selling its shares are unauthorized and invalid. The company states any stock transfers through these firms won't be recognized.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing sparks backlash among developers, while Goose offers identical AI coding capabilities as a free open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M in Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt generated thousands of applicants and successfully hired engineers.

articles.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
articles.summary: Linux faces a second critical vulnerability in weeks called Dirty Frag, allowing low-privilege users and containers to gain root access. Exploit code is publicly available and actively exploited across all Linux distributions.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals week, forcing Instructure to take it offline. The platform was restored Friday; user data including names and emails were accessed, but passwords remained secure.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detection tool Mythos identified 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives over two months. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's potential in software security, though Mozilla acknowledges previous hype and provides detailed methodology.

articles.title: World Models: 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
articles.summary: World models are an emerging AI area gaining significant attention for their potential to help AI systems better understand and reason about the real world. MIT Technology Review explores this technology's importance and implications for future AI systems.

articles.title: The Download: a Nobel winner on AI, and the case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Nobel-winning economist Daron Acemoglu argues AI will provide modest productivity gains and won't eliminate human work, a prediction supported by recent data despite tech industry skepticism.

articles.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
articles.summary: Nobel economist Daron Acemoglu argues AI will have modest productivity gains and won't eliminate white-collar work, contradicting tech industry predictions of massive job displacement.

articles.title: Building Blocks for Foundation Model Training and Inference on AWS
articles.summary: AWS provides building blocks for training and deploying foundation models, offering infrastructure and tools to streamline the development process for large-scale AI models.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a pretraining approach using mixture of experts to develop emergent modularity in AI models. This technique enables specialized neural modules to self-organize during training for improved efficiency.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM advances from V0 to V1 by prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before implementing corrections. The update focuses on establishing accurate foundational models for RL systems.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field. The conversation explores what's next for artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, providing tools to advance agricultural technology and sustainability through AI-driven farming solutions.

articles.title: The new AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe.
articles.summary: Google Finance's AI-powered platform is launching across Europe with full local language support, offering enhanced financial capabilities to users.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, pairing renowned advertising creatives with small businesses to create innovative AI-powered ads. The initiative aims to showcase how creative expertise combined with AI tools can elevate local businesses.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features offer five practical gardening tips to help plants thrive. The tools provide accessible resources for improving gardening success.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve, a Gemini-powered coding agent, scales impact across business, infrastructure, and science domains using advanced algorithms.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This new model aims to enhance patient care through AI collaboration with medical professionals.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to advance scientific discoveries through frontier AI models, accelerating research breakthroughs.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk's testimony dominated the first week of his trial against Sam Altman, with disputes over OpenAI's charitable origins and allegations of model theft between Musk's xAI and OpenAI.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and safety testing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model with better text rendering, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6. The episode covers recent advancements in multimodal AI capabilities.

articles.title: JBS Dev: On imperfect data and the AI last mile – from model capability to cost sustainability
articles.summary: JBS Dev's Joe Rose debunks the myth that perfect data is required for AI workloads, noting modern tools effectively handle imperfect data. LLMs can understand incomplete prompts, making it practical to begin AI projects without extensive data preparation.

articles.title: Hugging Face hosted malicious software masquerading as OpenAI release
articles.summary: A malicious Hugging Face repository impersonating OpenAI's Privacy Filter delivered infostealer malware to Windows machines, recording approximately 244,000 downloads before removal.

articles.title: Laserfiche unveils AI agents for natural language workflows
articles.summary: Laserfiche released AI agents that perform tasks through natural language prompts while maintaining security and compliance. These intelligent assistants automate workflows, reducing manual work and operational costs.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging and reliability of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses core challenges of localization and path planning in complex indoor environments, overcoming limitations of traditional rule-based systems.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Google brings multi-token prediction Gemma 4 LLMs
articles.summary: Google's Gemma 4 introduces multi-token prediction and DFlash technology, achieving 3-6x throughput improvements for local LLMs through community-driven development.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention enables large language models to handle 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, dramatically expanding context window capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are leaking sensitive API keys into public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Build a Hybrid-Memory Autonomous Agent with Modular Architecture and Tool Dispatch Using OpenAI
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a hybrid-memory autonomous agent using OpenAI with semantic vector search, keyword retrieval, and modular tool dispatch. System enables agents to reason, remember, and act autonomously with clean architecture design.

articles.title: Meet AntAngelMed: A 103B-Parameter Open-Source Medical Language Model Built on a 1/32 Activation-Ratio MoE Architecture
articles.summary: Chinese researchers released AntAngelMed, a 103-billion-parameter open-source medical language model using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture that activates only 6.1 billion parameters during inference.

articles.title: Tilde Research Introduces Aurora: A Leverage-Aware Optimizer That Fixes a Hidden Neuron Death Problem in Muon
articles.summary: Tilde Research introduced Aurora, a new neural network optimizer that fixes a neuron death problem in the popular Muon optimizer. Aurora achieves state-of-the-art results on benchmarks with improved performance.

articles.title: Fedora Kinoite vs. Silverblue: My verdict after testing both immutable Linux distros
articles.summary: Fedora offers two immutable Linux distros: Kinoite and Silverblue. While similar internally, they differ significantly in their external design and user experience approaches.

articles.title: Buying a robot mower? You can safely ignore these specs (and which ones actually matter)
articles.summary: Robot mowers often feature overhyped specifications that consumers don't need. The article recommends which specs to ignore when choosing the right mower for your needs.

articles.title: Chromebook vs. Googlebook: How I'm deciding which laptop to upgrade to next
articles.summary: Google launches a new product line while reassuring users that Chromebooks will continue. The company is expanding its laptop offerings beyond traditional Chromebook devices.

articles.title: LLM Observability Tools for Reliable AI Applications
articles.summary: LLM observability tools enable reliable AI applications as large language models increasingly power diverse use cases from customer service to autonomous coding.

articles.title: Implementing Prompt Compression to Reduce Agentic Loop Costs
articles.summary: Prompt compression reduces costs of agentic loops in production by minimizing token usage for LLM and API calls.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: This article discusses implementing permission-gated tool calling in Python agents, enabling AI agents to control access to specific functions based on authorization rules, moving beyond basic chatbot capabilities.

articles.title: From Vibe Coding to Spec-Driven Development
articles.summary: LLM agents enabled rapid fitness app development from concept to completion in 4.5 hours, demonstrating a shift from intuitive coding to specification-driven development approaches.

articles.title: Hybrid Search and Re-Ranking in Production RAG
articles.summary: Hybrid search and re-ranking techniques enhance RAG systems when semantic search alone proves insufficient for retrieving relevant information in production environments.

articles.title: Proxy-Pointer Framework for Structure-Aware Enterprise Document Intelligence
articles.summary: A new proxy-pointer framework enables hierarchical understanding and comparison of enterprise documents like contracts and research papers, improving document intelligence capabilities.

articles.title: NVIDIA and SAP Bring Trust to Specialized Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA and SAP collaborate to deploy trusted specialized AI agents in enterprise systems. SAP integrates NVIDIA's OpenShell into its Business AI Platform for secure agent development and governance.

articles.title: ‘Your Career Starts at the Beginning of the AI Revolution,’ NVIDIA CEO Tells Graduates
articles.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang told Carnegie Mellon graduates they are entering an extraordinary moment at the beginning of the AI revolution with unprecedented opportunities to shape the future.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that American AI leadership depends on energy leadership, with AI helping to build the energy infrastructure needed for the next century.

articles.title: How Amazon Finance streamlines regulatory inquiries by using generative AI on AWS
articles.summary: Amazon Finance uses generative AI on AWS Bedrock to streamline regulatory inquiries, automating document review, information extraction, and response compilation across multiple jurisdictions at scale.

articles.title: Automate schema generation for intelligent document processing
articles.summary: A new multi-document discovery feature automates schema generation for intelligent document processing by analyzing unknown documents, clustering them by type, and generating ready-to-use schemas using visual embeddings and AI agents.

articles.title: Navigating EU AI Act requirements for LLM fine-tuning on Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: The EU AI Act requires organizations fine-tuning LLMs to track computational resources in FLOPs to determine compliance. Amazon SageMaker AI provides managed infrastructure and tools to help customers monitor and meet these regulatory obligations.

articles.title: How to Eliminate Pipeline Friction in AI Model Serving
articles.summary: AI model deployment faces significant friction as teams encounter compatibility issues during export and production transitions. Optimizing pipelines with proper tools and formats can streamline the path from training to serving.

articles.title: Introducing NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence for Real-Time GPU Fleet Visibility and Optimization
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduces Fleet Intelligence for real-time GPU fleet visibility and optimization. The tool helps teams manage large-scale GPU deployments, addressing challenges with heterogeneous hardware, software stacks, and power management.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Researchers improve Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce valid executable shell commands for file operations and system tasks.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study explores how generational differences shape consumer attitudes toward social media ads across platforms. Understanding varying age groups' relationships with advertising helps inform targeted marketing strategies.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to achieving net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background successfully developed cutting-edge XR technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to innovation and breakthrough discoveries in immersive tech.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks, spawn concurrent threads, and coordinate them for efficient inference scaling.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through trajectory lifting, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping to improve optimization efficiency.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Research focuses on understanding Large Language Models' behavior through interpretability methods like feature attribution, aiming to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.
